rationale: A powerful show of force off the coast of Tethys will exploit their degraded command networks and signal our dominance amid fragile frontline stability.
Precision strikes against forward logistics hubs will disrupt enemy reinforcement capabilities while avoiding escalation to full-scale invasion.
Increased air patrols over contested regions will deter opportunistic advances and provide critical real-time intelligence on adversary movements.
Strengthening our own command networks is critical to prevent exploitation amid Tethys’ ongoing cyber disruptions and to ensure operational resilience during a fragile military stabilization.
A calibrated information campaign can erode international support for Tethys by highlighting their naval overreach and fabricated battlefield claims, exploiting the narrowing political window.
Given signs of reserve mobilization, we must deepen our human and signals intelligence to detect imminent troop movements that could disrupt the current frontline equilibrium.

Impose precision economic penalties on Tethyan defense contractors and offshore financial entities to exploit their weakened command systems and complement Krasnov’s naval demonstration with economic pressure.
Seize Tethyan state-linked assets in allied jurisdictions to disrupt their access to liquid capital, reinforcing Morozov’s cyber_defense by increasing the cost of their ongoing military posture.
Restrict dual-use technology exports to Tethys, limiting their ability to repair degraded military networks and indirectly supporting the information_campaign by highlighting their growing isolation.
A strong naval show near Serpent Island will exploit the recent vessel detention and signal resolve without escalating to open conflict.
Conducting visible drills along the frontline reinforces deterrence and prepares forces for potential escalation.
Persistent surveillance of enemy movements is critical to counter Novaris’ regrouping under the guise of stabilization.
Strengthening our military networks' resilience is critical to counter Novaris’ demonstrated cyber capabilities and ensure command continuity amid ongoing disruptions.
Proactively identifying and neutralizing Novaris intelligence operatives will protect our strategic plans and exploit their current overextension.
A targeted information campaign amplifies our legitimacy and leverages international support, reinforcing diplomatic pressure on Novaris without escalating hostilities.

Given moderate existing sanctions and high international support, we can credibly impose targeted sanctions on key Novaris defense-sector entities to amplify economic pressure without triggering broader retaliation.
Seizing assets of sanctioned Novaris oligarchs strengthens financial pressure and aligns with international efforts, enhancing our leverage while reinforcing rule-of-law credibility.
Restricting dual-use technology exports to Novaris complements military and intelligence actions by limiting their capacity to sustain operations and adapt to our cyber and naval initiatives.

Leveraging our high international support, we should launch a high-profile diplomatic campaign to reinforce the legitimacy of our position and isolate Novaris in global forums, in line with President Marchetti’s emphasis on institutional legitimacy.

Engaging through established international bodies will solidify Tethys’ role as a rules-based actor and complement General Bondar’s naval demonstration by anchoring it in legal and institutional credibility.
Promoting domestic and international cultural outreach will strengthen national resilience and counter Novaris’ narrative, subtly advancing our liberal-institutionalist values without provocation.
